description: This chart presents the dependence of austenite lattice parameter on Si and Al alloying in Fe-based austenitic steels. The x-axis represents relative alloying content in weight percent, while the y-axis shows austenite lattice parameter in nanometer. Three sample groups are included: the base austenitic steel (relative alloying content at 0, lattice parameter ~0.3585 nm), 2.5 wt.% Si alloyed steel (lattice parameter ~0.3587 nm, negligible difference from the base alloy), and 2.5 wt.% Al alloyed steel (lattice parameter ~0.3598 nm, significantly larger than the base alloy). The results demonstrate that Al alloying remarkably increases austenite lattice parameter, while Si alloying has minimal effect on this parameter. These experimental data support accurate lattice parameter-related calculations including stacking fault energy estimation and carbon content prediction for advanced high strength austenitic steel composition design.
